content: A2A protocols can be reimagined as a collective active learning memory system where each individual agent’s local memory is an active learning module that proactively queries other agents for missing information, rather than just statically exchanging pre-computed data. This turns inter-agent communication into a distributed, curiosity-driven memory consolidation process that improves overall system knowledge faster than isolated active learning on individual agents.
source_topics: memory as active learning agent SOTA 2025
